blocks.md: The **outer product** `∘.f` combines every element of the left with every element of the right, building a table. `(⍳3)∘.×⍳3` is a multiplication table.
Any scalar function works after `∘.`. With `+` you get an addition table; with `=` you get a table of matches — and an identity matrix falls out of `(⍳n)∘.=⍳n`.
The **inner product** `f.g` is the general matrix/vector product. `+.×` is the familiar dot product (multiply pairwise, then sum).

blocks.prompt: Build the 5×5 multiplication table from the first five integers.
Make a 4×4 identity matrix (1 on the diagonal, 0 elsewhere) using an outer product of equality.
blocks.hint: an element is 1 only where the row index equals the column index

blocks.prompt: Compute the dot product of 1 2 3 and 4 5 6. Expect 32.
blocks.hint: multiply element-wise then sum: the inner product +.×

blocks.prompt: Build the 3×3 addition table from the first three integers.
